summary: The suite covers all seven public functions and all stated requirements: plain-text and JSON version files, all three bump types (patch/minor/major via both '!' and BREAKING CHANGE body), 'none' bump, changelog creation/prepending, and the end-to-end orchestrator. Error paths (missing file, no version found, invalid format, unknown bump type) are exercised. Edge cases include 0.x.y versioning, mixed commit priority, whitespace trimming, and JSON field preservation. Gaps that drop rigor from 5: Get-BumpType is never called with an empty array; New-ChangelogEntry is not tested with majorCommitsBody to verify the 'Breaking Changes' section; there is no test verifying that a second Invoke-VersionBump call on an already-bumped changelog correctly prepends (integration-level append coverage). On design, commit-fixture variables are declared at script root rather than inside BeforeAll, which is fragile under Pester v5's scoping rules and could silently produce $null inside It blocks depending on configuration. Temp-file cleanup has no try/finally guard, so a test failure leaves orphaned files. BeforeEach/AfterEach would be more idiomatic for the file-system setup used in Set-Version and Invoke-VersionBump tests. Despite these issues, the suite is well-named, logically grouped with Context blocks, and would catch the majority of realistic regressions.
